title: 5 Bruins prospects who could push for a spot in Boston’s lineup next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Michael DiPietro, awarded AHL's top goalie, aims for a Bruins roster spot amid contract considerations.
Frederic Brunet, a promising defenseman, could boost Bruins' lineup with his offensive skills and size.
Matt Poitras and Dans Locmelis target NHL slots after respective successes in AHL and international play.

title: Lawrence man charged with murder after woman dies from serious head injury
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Tyler Halley, 21, charged with murder after a woman was found dead with a severe head wound in Lawrence.
The woman was unresponsive and later died at Merrimack Health Lawrence Hospital; her identity remains undisclosed.
Lawrence police, State Police, and the Medical Examiner are investigating the woman's death and Halley's involvement.

title: ‘Purest values of service’: Communities pay tribute after veteran Boston firefighter, Marine veteran killed battling Dorchester blaze
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Veteran Boston firefighter and Marine, Robert Kilduff Jr., died battling a blaze at a Dorchester residence Saturday night.
Kilduff, a third-generation firefighter, was seriously injured after falling from a third-story window and later died at Boston Medical Center.
Kilduff's wake will be held May 31 at St. Theresa of Avila Parish; his funeral is scheduled for June 1 at Cathedral of the Holy Cross.

title: Jaylen Brown challenges Stephen A. Smith to debate at site like Harvard or MIT
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown challenges Stephen A. Smith to a live debate at Harvard or MIT over media industry concerns.
Brown and Smith's feud began after Boston's playoff loss, with ongoing criticism shared via Twitch and ESPN.
Smith warns Brown about potential repercussions from media coverage of his personal and professional life.

title: NBA Insider: Celtics, Heat are 2 teams ‘believed to intrigue’ Giannis in a blockbuster trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Celtics and Heat are potential destinations for Giannis Antetokounmpo, according to NBA insider Marc Stein.
Trading for Giannis may require the Celtics to part with Jaylen Brown in a three-team deal.
Atlanta Hawks, Houston Rockets, and Portland Trail Blazers are interested in acquiring Jaylen Brown.

title: Read what Bruce Springsteen had to say about Trump, ICE, and America in Boston Sunday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized President Trump's administration during his concert at TD Garden in Boston.
Springsteen highlighted issues like the Voting Rights Act, ICE, and U.S. foreign relations in his statements.
He praised the solidarity of Minneapolis residents and remembered individuals like Renee Good and Alex Pretti.

title: Review and setlist: Springsteen eviscerates Trump at fiery TD Garden stop
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized President Trump during his TD Garden concert, using music to highlight political and social issues.
The E Street Band's performance featured 27 songs, including classics and political anthems, plus guest guitarist Tom Morello.
Springsteen's setlist included tracks like 'Born in the USA' and 'Death to My Hometown,' emphasizing themes of resistance and unity.

title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, celebrating American music genres.
Odom Jr. sang songs like 'Fly Me To The Moon' and a medley of Hamilton hits with the Pops.
The concert was part of the Boston Pops' 2026 spring season, marking their 140th anniversary.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, faces a dozen charges for assaulting two children under 14 in Chelsea, Massachusetts.
Bautista-Zepeda allegedly used a lighter and a heated fork to burn the children, causing injury, according to prosecutors.
Authorities arrested Bautista-Zepeda in Virginia after escaping; he awaits a dangerousness hearing on May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in July 2023.
Jammal received a prison sentence of 13 ½ to 15 years and 5 years of probation.
Victim encountered Jammal in hallway; he assaulted her and followed her into the restroom.
